_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 10-16 SPRN Medical Isolation Transport Device (MDHHS Reviewed 2022-12-27). MCA Optional — SPRN program participation required.
protocol_reference: Michigan Special Operations Protocol Section 10-16: SPRN Medical Isolation Transport Device — MDHHS Reviewed 2022
purpose: Define the Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD) — its description, indications, setup, and patient handoff procedures — for use in SPRN Type 3 (High Risk/Wet) patient transport.

sections.title: MITD Description
content: Medical Isolation Transport Device (MITD): a specialized containment device designed to isolate infectious patients during ambulance transport
Construction: vinyl-enclosed transparent containment unit — allows visual patient assessment through clear panels
Pressure: NEGATIVE air pressure — air flows INTO the device, not outward; exhaled air filtered through HEPA before exiting
Purpose: contain infectious aerosols and body fluids to prevent EMS crew exposure during transport
Also known as: Biocell, Isolation Pod, HCID transport containment unit
MITD is REQUIRED for SPRN Type 3 (High Risk/Wet) patients — see Protocol 10-11

sections.title: MITD Indications
content: REQUIRED: SPRN Type 3 patient (High Risk/Wet) — active bleeding, vomiting, diarrhea, or significant body fluid exposure risk
OPTIONAL/RECOMMENDED: SPRN Type 1 (PUI) with significant secretion risk
NOT REQUIRED: SPRN Type 2 (Low Risk/Dry) patient — full PPE sufficient without MITD
If uncertain of patient type: use MITD — safer to over-contain than under-contain

sections.title: Equipment Placement and Setup
content: Setup MITD before patient is loaded — assemble at patient pickup location (sending facility)
MITD assembly steps:
1. Lay flat on ambulance stretcher and unfold according to device instructions
2. Connect blower motor to power supply and MITD intake/exhaust ports
3. Test blower motor function — confirm negative pressure is achieved inside device
4. Route IV lines, cardiac monitor leads, SpO2 cable, and ventilator tubing through designated ACCESS PORTS before patient placement
5. Confirm port seals are intact around all cables/tubes
Equipment placement:
— IV pumps, cardiac monitor, ventilator: OUTSIDE the MITD with lines/leads passing through ports
— Patient: inside the MITD
— SpO2 probe: attached to patient inside; cable exits through port to monitor outside
— Defibrillator pads: apply to patient inside MITD before sealing; lead cable through port
Access during transport: avoid entering MITD during transport; all care delivered through access ports with gloved hands

sections.title: Blower Motor Setup
content: Blower motor creates and maintains negative pressure inside MITD
Setup requirements:
— Connect to vehicle power or dedicated battery — confirm power source before departure
— HEPA filtration on exhaust port — exhaled air filtered before release into ambulance
— Monitor blower motor function continuously during transport — audible/visual indicator
— Backup battery required for long transports (>2 hours)
Blower motor failure during transport:
— Notify partner immediately
— Contact SPRN coordinator
— If failure cannot be immediately corrected: continue transport at maximum speed to destination with providers maintaining maximum PPE

sections.title: Patient Placement in MITD
content: Place patient in MITD at sending facility BEFORE loading into ambulance
Patient position: supine on stretcher; strapped in as per standard ambulance transport protocol
Patient comfort: explain device purpose to patient; provide pillow, communication method (patient can speak through vinyl)
Airway: if patient requires oxygen — route nasal cannula or mask through device port before sealing; do NOT use oxygen inside MITD without routing exhaust
Seal all access ports after equipment routing — inspect for gaps
Do NOT open MITD during transport unless immediate life-threatening emergency — access ports are designed for all routine interventions

sections.title: Patient Handoff — Two-Team Process
content: SPRN patient handoff at receiving facility is a TWO-TEAM process:
TEAM 1 (EMS — 'dirty' team): providers in full SPRN PPE who have been in patient contact
— Deliver MITD with patient to receiving facility SPRN area
— Provide verbal report to receiving team
— Transfer MITD to hospital gurney or isolation room
TEAM 2 (Hospital — 'receiving' team): hospital SPRN team in full PPE at receiving area
— Receive MITD from EMS team
— Take custody of patient and MITD in isolation room
HANDOFF STEPS:
1. EMS notifies hospital 10-15 min before arrival (Protocol 10-13)
2. EMS arrives at designated SPRN entrance
3. EMS team (full PPE) wheels patient to designated isolation area
4. Brief verbal report
5. Hospital team takes custody — EMS team does NOT enter hospital isolation room
6. EMS team proceeds to designated doffing area
7. Doff per Protocol 10-17 with safety officer
8. Post-transport health monitoring per Protocol 10-17

mca_selections_required.label: SPRN Program Participation — MCA authorization for SPRN protocols 10-10 through 10-18
mca_selections_required.description: MCA Optional: All SPRN protocols apply only to SPRN-designated agencies.

deterioration_flags: Blower motor failure — maximum PPE; continue transport; contact SPRN coordinator
Access port seal failure (contamination breach) — alert partner; proceed to receiving facility; report breach per Protocol 10-14 PPE breach protocol
Patient clinical emergency requiring MITD opening — extreme caution; full PPE; contact online MC before opening
scope_notes: MCA Optional — SPRN only. MITD is required for Type 3 (High Risk/Wet) patients only. All medical equipment (monitors, IV pumps, defibrillator) is positioned OUTSIDE the MITD with cables passing through sealed ports. Patient handoff is a formal two-team process — EMS does NOT enter the receiving hospital isolation room.
